description: Capture and document standards from conversation feedback into LESSONS_LEARNED.md

evals.prompt: User statement: 'Ok, make sure the playwright/chrome is installed and works. Test it on a sample website of your choice, you need to record on the lessons learned that the user expects that the pre-requisites are installed, and confirm by testing them before saying the task is ready.' Now capture this into LESSONS_LEARNED.md under the Prerequisites & Validation section.
evals.expected_output: A LESSONS_LEARNED.md file with a new section under Prerequisites & Validation containing: Standard (verify prerequisites before claiming readiness), Context (user expects Playwright/Chrome installed and working), Action (test prerequisites on sample website first), Priority (HIGH), Enforced (Manual verification required). File should be machine-readable YAML + Markdown.

evals.prompt: User says during a task: 'Wait, before you create the file, check if there's already one. Don't overwrite existing work.' Capture this correction as a new standard in the Process Standards section of LESSONS_LEARNED.md.
evals.expected_output: Added to Process Standards: 'File Safety' section with Standard (check if file exists before creating/overwriting), Context (prevent loss of existing work), Action (search for file first, ask before overwriting), Priority (HIGH). Timestamp updated in YAML header.

evals.prompt: Start with a LESSONS_LEARNED.md that has 3 existing standards. Now add 2 new standards from conversation feedback. Verify that: 1) All original standards are preserved, 2) New standards are added with context, 3) Version number incremented, 4) Timestamp updated. Show the result.
evals.expected_output: LESSONS_LEARNED.md with all 5 standards visible, original ones unchanged, new ones formatted consistently. YAML version incremented (e.g., 1.0 → 1.1). last_updated timestamp reflects current time. No standards deleted or modified unless explicitly requested.

evals.prompt: Given a LESSONS_LEARNED.md with 5 standards (2 marked HIGH, 3 MEDIUM), write a summary showing: 1) How a coding agent would discover HIGH-priority standards, 2) What actions it must perform before proceeding, 3) How it reports compliance. Include the specific standards extracted.
evals.expected_output: Summary showing parsed YAML metadata, identified 2 HIGH-priority standards with their Actions extracted, compliance checklist agent would follow, and example report output like 'Following project standards: ✓ Checked prerequisite X, ✓ Tested Y...'

evals.prompt: Design a minimal project config (.copilot-config.json or similar) that: 1) Points to LESSONS_LEARNED.md, 2) Enables auto-loading, 3) Specifies categories, 4) Includes agent instructions for before/during/after task. Show how an agent would use this config to enforce standards automatically.
evals.expected_output: JSON/YAML config file with lessons_learned section, auto_load enabled, categories list, pre_task/during_task/post_task agent instructions. Example showing agent flow: (startup) load config → parse LESSONS_LEARNED.md → report relevant standards → apply actions before proceeding.

evals.prompt: Create a section of LESSONS_LEARNED.md (e.g., 'Code Review Standards') that is equally readable by: 1) A human scanning the file, 2) A YAML/regex parser extracting standards, 3) A semantic AI parser understanding context. Show that parsing works without special markup or conventions.
evals.expected_output: Section with standard structure (###title, **Standard**, **Context**, **Action**, **Priority**, **Enforced**). Demonstrate successful parsing via: human-readable output, YAML-valid extraction, and semantic extraction of intent. All three parsing methods return the same standard details.

evals.prompt: User emphasizes the same standard twice in conversation (e.g., 'Never skip testing' early, then later 'Remember, no task is done without testing'). Capture this into LESSONS_LEARNED.md handling repetition: 1) Detect it's the same standard, 2) Don't duplicate it, 3) Merge context/actions if they differ, 4) Update relevant parts only. Show result.
evals.expected_output: LESSONS_LEARNED.md with single consolidated 'Validation Before Completion' standard, not duplicated. If second mention added context, show merged version. Timestamp updated only once. Explanation of how repetition was detected and consolidated.

evals.prompt: Capture a standard that applies across multiple categories (e.g., 'Test everything' applies to both 'process' and 'quality-gates'). Show how to: 1) Record it without duplication, 2) Cross-reference it, 3) Ensure agents see it in both categories when parsing.
evals.expected_output: Standard recorded in primary category with clear reference/link to related categories. Example: '### Testing Standard' in quality-gates section with note '(See also: Process Standards > Validation Before Completion)'. Agent parser can find standard across category boundaries.
